title: Understand: sensory processing and the brain
description: Check your understanding of sensory processing and the brain in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Understand: sensory processing and the brain

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the name for a cell or cell part that detects signals from the environment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A \_\_\_\_\_\_ is a cell or cell part that detects signals from the environment.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a signal from the environment that can lead to a response in the body
a cell or cell part that detects signals from the environment
a memory formed as a result of sensory input

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these senses results from the detection of mechanical stimuli?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these senses results from the detection of chemical stimuli?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these senses results from the detection of electromagnetic stimuli?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these tasks are carried out by the brain?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: triggering a response to sensory information

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these tasks are carried out by the brain?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which sentence best describes the role of the brain in sensory processing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The brain processes sensory information, and then triggers a response and makes a memory.
The brain directly detects signals from the environment.
The brain helps transmit signals from the environment to the nervous system.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following steps related to sensory processing occurs *first*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The brain triggers a response and stores a memory.
Sensory receptors detect signals from the environment.
Information from receptors is passed along nerve cells to the brain where the information is organized.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following steps related to sensory processing occurs *first*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sensory receptors pass information along nerve cells to the brain where the information is organized.
Signals from the environment are detected by the body’s sensory receptors.
The brain triggers a response and stores a memory.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following steps related to sensory processing occurs *last*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sensory receptors detect signals from the environment.
The brain triggers a response and stores a memory.
Information from receptors is passed along nerve cells to the brain where the information is organized.
